members.name: GetPlaybackPosition
members.summary: Returns the position in the [AudioStream].

members.name: GetStreamPlayback
members.summary: Returns the [AudioStreamPlayback] object associated with this [AudioStreamPlayer3D].

members.name: HasStreamPlayback
members.summary: Returns whether the [AudioStreamPlayer] can return the [AudioStreamPlayback] object or not.

members.name: Play
members.summary: Queues the audio to play on the next physics frame, from the given position [param from_position], in seconds.

members.name: Seek
members.summary: Sets the position from which audio will be played, in seconds.

members.name: Stop
members.summary: Stops the audio.

members.name: AreaMask
members.summary: Determines which [Area3D] layers affect the sound for reverb and audio bus effects. Areas can be used to redirect [AudioStream]s so that they play in a certain audio bus. An example of how you might use this is making a "water" area so that sounds played in the water are redirected through an audio bus to make them sound like they are being played underwater.

members.name: AttenuationFilterCutoffHz
members.summary: The cutoff frequency of the attenuation low-pass filter, in Hz. A sound above this frequency is attenuated more than a sound below this frequency. To disable this effect, set this to [code]20500[/code] as this frequency is above the human hearing limit.

members.name: AttenuationFilterDb
members.summary: Amount how much the filter affects the loudness, in decibels.

members.name: AttenuationModel
members.summary: Decides if audio should get quieter with distance linearly, quadratically, logarithmically, or not be affected by distance, effectively disabling attenuation.

members.name: Autoplay
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], audio plays when the AudioStreamPlayer3D node is added to scene tree.

members.name: Bus
members.summary: The bus on which this audio is playing. [b]Note:[/b] When setting this property, keep in mind that no validation is performed to see if the given name matches an existing bus. This is because audio bus layouts might be loaded after this property is set. If this given name can't be resolved at runtime, it will fall back to [code]"Master"[/code].

members.name: DopplerTracking
members.summary: Decides in which step the Doppler effect should be calculated. [b]Note:[/b] If [member doppler_tracking] is not [constant DOPPLER_TRACKING_DISABLED] but the current [Camera3D]/[AudioListener3D] has doppler tracking disabled, the Doppler effect will be heard but will not take the movement of the current listener into account. If accurate Doppler effect is desired, doppler tracking should be enabled on both the [AudioStreamPlayer3D] and the current [Camera3D]/[AudioListener3D].

members.name: EmissionAngleDegrees
members.summary: The angle in which the audio reaches a listener unattenuated.

members.name: EmissionAngleEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the audio should be attenuated according to the direction of the sound.

members.name: EmissionAngleFilterAttenuationDb
members.summary: Attenuation factor used if listener is outside of [member emission_angle_degrees] and [member emission_angle_enabled] is set, in decibels.

members.name: MaxDb
members.summary: Sets the absolute maximum of the sound level, in decibels.

members.name: MaxDistance
members.summary: The distance past which the sound can no longer be heard at all. Only has an effect if set to a value greater than [code]0.0[/code]. [member max_distance] works in tandem with [member unit_size]. However, unlike [member unit_size] whose behavior depends on the [member attenuation_model], [member max_distance] always works in a linear fashion. This can be used to prevent the [AudioStreamPlayer3D] from requiring audio mixing when the listener is far away, which saves CPU resources.

members.name: MaxPolyphony
members.summary: The maximum number of sounds this node can play at the same time. Playing additional sounds after this value is reached will cut off the oldest sounds.

members.name: PanningStrength
members.summary: Scales the panning strength for this node by multiplying the base [member ProjectSettings.audio/general/3d_panning_strength] by this factor. If the product is [code]0.0[/code] then stereo panning is disabled and the volume is the same for all channels. If the product is [code]1.0[/code] then one of the channels will be muted when the sound is located exactly to the left (or right) of the listener. Two speaker stereo arrangements implement the [url=https://webaudio.github.io/web-audio-api/#stereopanner-algorithm]WebAudio standard for StereoPannerNode Panning[/url] where the volume is cosine of half the azimuth angle to the ear. For other speaker arrangements such as the 5.1 and 7.1 the SPCAP (Speaker-Placement Correction Amplitude) algorithm is implemented.

members.name: PitchScale
members.summary: The pitch and the tempo of the audio, as a multiplier of the audio sample's sample rate.

members.name: PlaybackType
members.summary: The playback type of the stream player. If set other than to the default value, it will force that playback type.

members.name: Playing
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], audio is playing or is queued to be played (see [method play]).

members.name: Stream
members.summary: The [AudioStream] resource to be played.

members.name: StreamPaused
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the playback is paused. You can resume it by setting [member stream_paused] to [code]false[/code].

members.name: UnitSize
members.summary: The factor for the attenuation effect. Higher values make the sound audible over a larger distance.

members.name: VolumeDb
members.summary: The base sound level before attenuation, in decibels.

members.name: VolumeLinear
members.summary: The base sound level before attenuation, as a linear value. [b]Note:[/b] This member modifies [member volume_db] for convenience. The returned value is equivalent to the result of [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] on [member volume_db]. Setting this member is equivalent to setting [member volume_db] to the result of [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db] on a value.

signals.name: Finished
signals.summary: Emitted when the audio stops playing.

values.name: ATTENUATION_DISABLED
values.summary: No attenuation of loudness according to distance. The sound will still be heard positionally, unlike an [AudioStreamPlayer]. [constant ATTENUATION_DISABLED] can be combined with a [member max_distance] value greater than [code]0.0[/code] to achieve linear attenuation clamped to a sphere of a defined size.

values.name: ATTENUATION_INVERSE_DISTANCE
values.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to linear distance.

values.name: ATTENUATION_INVERSE_SQUARE_DISTANCE
values.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to squared distance.

values.name: ATTENUATION_LOGARITHMIC
values.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to logarithmic distance.

values.name: DOPPLER_TRACKING_IDLE_STEP
values.summary: Executes doppler tracking during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).

values.name: DOPPLER_TRACKING_PHYSICS_STEP
values.summary: Executes doppler tracking during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).

constants.name: ATTENUATION_INVERSE_DISTANCE
constants.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to linear distance.

constants.name: ATTENUATION_INVERSE_SQUARE_DISTANCE
constants.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to squared distance.

constants.name: ATTENUATION_LOGARITHMIC
constants.summary: Attenuation of loudness according to logarithmic distance.

constants.name: ATTENUATION_DISABLED
constants.summary: No attenuation of loudness according to distance. The sound will still be heard positionally, unlike an [AudioStreamPlayer]. [constant ATTENUATION_DISABLED] can be combined with a [member max_distance] value greater than [code]0.0[/code] to achieve linear attenuation clamped to a sphere of a defined size.

constants.name: DOPPLER_TRACKING_IDLE_STEP
constants.summary: Executes doppler tracking during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).

constants.name: DOPPLER_TRACKING_PHYSICS_STEP
constants.summary: Executes doppler tracking during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).
